BY NO LATER THAN APRIL 19, 2019 PARTIES TO FILE SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFS ADDRESSING THE FOLLOWING QUESTIONS REGARDING "UTILIZING PARTNERS" OCCUPYING UNITS LOCATED IN SAN FRANCISCO: 1. ARE UTILIZING PARTNERS "TENANTS": (A) PER COMMON AND STATE LAW; AND (2) PER SFRO? 2. DID THE ARBITRATOR DETERMINE WHETHER UTILIZING PARTNERS ARE "TENANTS" (A) PER COMMON AND STATE LAW; AND (B) PER SFRO? 3. DOES SFRO APPLY TO THESE TENANCIES? 4. IS THERE A DETERMINATION BY THE ARBITRATOR WHETHER SFRO APPLIES TO THESE TENANCIES? IF YES, WHAT IS THE DETERMINATION? 5. ASSUMING THAT SFRO PROTECTIONS APPLY, MAY THE PARTIES CONTRACT AROUND THE RENT INCREASE LIMITATIONS? IN THE BRIEF INCLUDE THE QUESTION FOLLOWED BY "YES" OR "NO" RESPONSE PRIOR TO ANY ARGUMENT. INCLUDE ALL THE RELEVANT AUTHORITY ON POINT AND CITATIONS TO RELEVANT SENTENCES FROM THE AWARD. SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFS SHALL NOT EXCEED 2 PAGES. NO FURTHER BRIEFING IS ALLOWED. JUDGE CHARLES F. Motion For Order Appointing Receiver
Off Calendar Apr-28-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
William Castillo Guardado, Esq., 510-663-3700, appearing for Lonette Robinso, Plaintiff

Robert Peterson, Esq., 415-586-7460, appearing for Larua Perkins, for Defendant.

ARGUED. Mr. Guardado stated that the motion was filed due to the lack of communication with LaRue, that they would like accountability, that there has been no word of finances, that the actions of LaRue as manager has been harmful to the property and that a receiver is appropriate. Mr. Peterson argued that LaRue's actions have been beneficial, that Mr. Bower is angry and owes $18,000.00 and that all of the partners are in agreement of a new receiver. The Court stated that there is a lack of accounting, that an accounting is not a requirement of the settlement, that the term of the manager cannot be extended and suggested to let LaRue's terms end naturally. Mr. Guardado asked Mr. Peterson if he can agree that LaRue's terms end in June 2022 and requested an accounting. Mr. Peterson stated no he does not agree and reported that the receiver collected some outstanding funds but some funds are still owed to the partnership.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER KERRY BOWSER Motion For Order Appointing Receiver is DENIED. If the parties cannot agree on an internal manager or a third-party manager once Respondent LaRue Perkins' two-year term as Manager ends, the parties may refile the motion. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Hearing
Off Calendar Apr-02-2019 Continued To Apr-26-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENTS LARUE PERKINS, PATRICIA ANDERSON, and BETTY BOWSER'S Notice Of Hearing

is continued to April 26, 2019. By no later than April 19, 2019 parties to file supplemental briefs addressing the following questions regarding "utilizing partners" occupying units located in San Francisco:
1. Are utilizing partners "tenants": (a) per common and state law; and (2) per SFRO?
2. Did the arbitrator determine whether utilizing partners are "tenants" (a) per common and state law; and (b) per SFRO?
3. Does SFRO apply to these tenancies?
4. Is there a determination by the arbitrator whether SFRO applies to these tenancies? If yes, what is the determination?
5. Assuming that SFRO protections apply, may the parties contract around the rent increase limitations?
In the brief include the question followed by "Yes" or "No" response prior to any argument. Include all the relevant authority on point and citations to relevant sentences from the Award. Supplemental briefs shall not exceed 2 pages. No further briefing is allowed.

Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported (D501)
